Voices.description: A list of voices with their properties.

NextToken.description: The pagination token to use in the next request to continue the listing of voices. NextToken is returned only if the response is truncated.

Id.description: Amazon Polly assigned voice ID. This is the ID that you specify when calling the SynthesizeSpeech operation.

LanguageName.description: Human readable name of the language in English.

Name.description: Name of the voice (for example, Salli, Kendra, etc.). This provides a human readable voice name that you might display in your application.

AdditionalLanguageCodes.description:
Additional codes for languages available for the specified voice in addition to its default language.

For example, the default language for Aditi is Indian English (en-IN) because it was first used for that language. Since Aditi is bilingual and fluent in both Indian English and Hindi, this parameter would show the code hi-IN.

SupportedEngines.description: Specifies which engines (standard, neural, long-form or generative) are supported by a given voice.

Lexicons.description: A list of lexicon names and attributes.

NextToken.description: The pagination token to use in the next request to continue the listing of lexicons. NextToken is returned only if the response is truncated.

Alphabet.description: Phonetic alphabet used in the lexicon. Valid values are ipa and x-sampa.

LanguageCode.description: Language code that the lexicon applies to. A lexicon with a language code such as "en" would be applied to all English languages (en-GB, en-US, en-AUS, en-WLS, and so on.

LastModified.description: Date lexicon was last modified (a timestamp value).

Size.description: Total size of the lexicon, in characters.

NextToken.description: An opaque pagination token returned from the previous List operation in this request. If present, this indicates where to continue the listing.

SynthesisTasks.description: List of SynthesisTask objects that provides information from the specified task in the list request, including output format, creation time, task status, and so on.

Engine.description: Specifies the engine (standard, neural, long-form or generative) for Amazon Polly to use when processing input text for speech synthesis. Using a voice that is not supported for the engine selected will result in an error.

TaskId.description: The Amazon Polly generated identifier for a speech synthesis task.

TaskStatus.description: Current status of the individual speech synthesis task.

TaskStatusReason.description: Reason for the current status of a specific speech synthesis task, including errors if the task has failed.

CreationTime.description: Timestamp for the time the synthesis task was started.

SnsTopicArn.description: ARN for the SNS topic optionally used for providing status notification for a speech synthesis task.

LexiconNames.description: List of one or more pronunciation lexicon names you want the service to apply during synthesis. Lexicons are applied only if the language of the lexicon is the same as the language of the voice.

OutputFormat.description: The format in which the returned output will be encoded. For audio stream, this will be mp3, ogg_vorbis, or pcm. For speech marks, this will be json.

SampleRate.description:
The audio frequency specified in Hz.

The valid values for mp3 and ogg_vorbis are "8000", "16000", "22050", and "24000". The default value for standard voices is "22050". The default value for neural voices is "24000". The default value for long-form voices is "24000". The default value for generative voices is "24000".

Valid values for pcm are "8000" and "16000" The default value is "16000".

SpeechMarkTypes.description: The type of speech marks returned for the input text.

TextType.description: Specifies whether the input text is plain text or SSML. The default value is plain text.

VoiceId.description: Voice ID to use for the synthesis.

LanguageCode.description:
Optional language code for a synthesis task. This is only necessary if using a bilingual voice, such as Aditi, which can be used for either Indian English (en-IN) or Hindi (hi-IN).

If a bilingual voice is used and no language code is specified, Amazon Polly uses the default language of the bilingual voice. The default language for any voice is the one returned by the DescribeVoices operation for the LanguageCode parameter. For example, if no language code is specified, Aditi will use Indian English rather than Hindi.

Lexicon.description: Lexicon object that provides name and the string content of the lexicon.

LexiconAttributes.description: Metadata of the lexicon, including phonetic alphabetic used, language code, lexicon ARN, number of lexemes defined in the lexicon, and size of lexicon in bytes.

Content.description: Lexicon content in string format. The content of a lexicon must be in PLS format.

SynthesisTask.description: SynthesisTask object that provides information from the requested task, including output format, creation time, task status, and so on.

Engine.description: Specifies the engine (standard, neural, long-form or generative) for Amazon Polly to use when processing input text for speech synthesis. Using a voice that is not supported for the engine selected will result in an error.

TaskId.description: The Amazon Polly generated identifier for a speech synthesis task.

TaskStatus.description: Current status of the individual speech synthesis task.

TaskStatusReason.description: Reason for the current status of a specific speech synthesis task, including errors if the task has failed.

CreationTime.description: Timestamp for the time the synthesis task was started.

SnsTopicArn.description: ARN for the SNS topic optionally used for providing status notification for a speech synthesis task.

LexiconNames.description: List of one or more pronunciation lexicon names you want the service to apply during synthesis. Lexicons are applied only if the language of the lexicon is the same as the language of the voice.

OutputFormat.description: The format in which the returned output will be encoded. For audio stream, this will be mp3, ogg_vorbis, or pcm. For speech marks, this will be json.

SampleRate.description:
The audio frequency specified in Hz.

The valid values for mp3 and ogg_vorbis are "8000", "16000", "22050", and "24000". The default value for standard voices is "22050". The default value for neural voices is "24000". The default value for long-form voices is "24000". The default value for generative voices is "24000".

Valid values for pcm are "8000" and "16000" The default value is "16000".

SpeechMarkTypes.description: The type of speech marks returned for the input text.

TextType.description: Specifies whether the input text is plain text or SSML. The default value is plain text.

VoiceId.description: Voice ID to use for the synthesis.

LanguageCode.description:
Optional language code for a synthesis task. This is only necessary if using a bilingual voice, such as Aditi, which can be used for either Indian English (en-IN) or Hindi (hi-IN).

If a bilingual voice is used and no language code is specified, Amazon Polly uses the default language of the bilingual voice. The default language for any voice is the one returned by the DescribeVoices operation for the LanguageCode parameter. For example, if no language code is specified, Aditi will use Indian English rather than Hindi.

SynthesisTask.description: SynthesisTask object that provides information and attributes about a newly submitted speech synthesis task.

Engine.description: Specifies the engine (standard, neural, long-form or generative) for Amazon Polly to use when processing input text for speech synthesis. Using a voice that is not supported for the engine selected will result in an error.

TaskId.description: The Amazon Polly generated identifier for a speech synthesis task.

TaskStatus.description: Current status of the individual speech synthesis task.

TaskStatusReason.description: Reason for the current status of a specific speech synthesis task, including errors if the task has failed.

CreationTime.description: Timestamp for the time the synthesis task was started.

SnsTopicArn.description: ARN for the SNS topic optionally used for providing status notification for a speech synthesis task.

LexiconNames.description: List of one or more pronunciation lexicon names you want the service to apply during synthesis. Lexicons are applied only if the language of the lexicon is the same as the language of the voice.

OutputFormat.description: The format in which the returned output will be encoded. For audio stream, this will be mp3, ogg_vorbis, or pcm. For speech marks, this will be json.

SampleRate.description:
The audio frequency specified in Hz.

The valid values for mp3 and ogg_vorbis are "8000", "16000", "22050", and "24000". The default value for standard voices is "22050". The default value for neural voices is "24000". The default value for long-form voices is "24000". The default value for generative voices is "24000".

Valid values for pcm are "8000" and "16000" The default value is "16000".

SpeechMarkTypes.description: The type of speech marks returned for the input text.

TextType.description: Specifies whether the input text is plain text or SSML. The default value is plain text.

VoiceId.description: Voice ID to use for the synthesis.

LanguageCode.description:
Optional language code for a synthesis task. This is only necessary if using a bilingual voice, such as Aditi, which can be used for either Indian English (en-IN) or Hindi (hi-IN).

If a bilingual voice is used and no language code is specified, Amazon Polly uses the default language of the bilingual voice. The default language for any voice is the one returned by the DescribeVoices operation for the LanguageCode parameter. For example, if no language code is specified, Aditi will use Indian English rather than Hindi.

ContentType.description:
Specifies the type audio stream. This should reflect the OutputFormat parameter in your request.

  • If you request mp3 as the OutputFormat, the ContentType returned is audio/mpeg.

  • If you request ogg_vorbis as the OutputFormat, the ContentType returned is audio/ogg.

  • If you request pcm as the OutputFormat, the ContentType returned is audio/pcm in a signed 16-bit, 1 channel (mono), little-endian format.

  • If you request json as the OutputFormat, the ContentType returned is application/x-json-stream.
